module-2-community-website-history.label: Project: Build a Readable Website History
module-2-community-website-history.summary: A community website has two unrelated edits. Separate them into focused snapshots, then read the history as a clear project story.

sketch0.title: Build a History Another Maintainer Can Read

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Read and Verify the Finished History
Inspect the Two Pending Changes
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

The website has a navigation edit and a palette edit. Use status, name-only diff, and a focused diff of `index.html` to understand the work before staging.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: A project begins with inspection so commit boundaries are chosen from evidence, not guesswork.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git status --short
git diff --name-only
git diff -- index.html
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

The website has a navigation edit and a palette edit. Use status, name-only diff, and a focused diff of `index.html` to understand the work before staging.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf community-website mkdir -p community-website git -C community-website init -q -b main git -C community-website config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner" git -C community-website config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local" cd community-website printf %s '

Community Hub

Home
' > index.html cd .. cd community-website printf %s 'body { color: navy; } ' > styles.css cd .. git -C community-website add -A git -C community-website commit -q -m 'Add project baseline' cd community-website printf %s '

Community Hub

Home Events
' > index.html cd .. cd community-website printf %s 'body { color: teal; } ' > styles.css cd ..

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Stage the Navigation Update
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

Stage only `index.html`, then inspect the staged file names and patch. Leave the palette edit unstaged.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The index can hold one focused part of a larger working tree.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git add index.html
git diff --staged --name-only
git diff --staged -- index.html

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Commit the Navigation Update
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

The navigation change is staged and the palette edit remains unstaged. Commit `Add events navigation`, then verify the remaining status.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: A commit records only the staged navigation work and leaves the unrelated palette edit for its own snapshot.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Add events navigation"
git status --short
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions:

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Stage the Palette Update
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

The navigation commit now exists. Stage `styles.css` and inspect the staged patch.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The second coherent change receives its own staging boundary after the first snapshot is complete.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git add styles.css
git diff --staged -- styles.css

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

The palette change is staged. Commit `Refresh site palette` and verify a clean working tree.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: Separate commits make navigation and design changes independently understandable.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

git status --short

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Read the Finished Website History
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt:
The terminal opens inside the prepared `community-website/` repository.

Read the three one-line entries, inspect the latest snapshot with `git show --stat HEAD`, and confirm the working tree remains clean.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The final history explains the project in three focused milestones and remains safe to inspect.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git log --oneline
git show --stat HEAD
git status --short

intro-sketch.title: Build a History Another Maintainer Can Read
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown:
The community center website needs two unrelated updates: navigation and weekend events. Recording both in one large snapshot would hide the reason for each change.

You will inspect each diff, stage only the files for the current purpose, create two focused commits, and read the finished timeline. The project carries the repository state forward between steps while keeping setup invisible.

The final history should explain itself without opening every file.
